findings.title: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack
findings.description: The HTTP/2 protocol allows a denial of service (server resource consumption) because request cancellation can reset many streams quickly, as exploited in the wild in August through October 2023.

findings.title: OCI Registry Distribution Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
findings.description: A flaw was found in the distribution/distribution package. A specially crafted input to the reference.ParseNamed() function allows attackers to cause unexpected memory consumption.

findings.title: Golang net/http HTTP/2 header flood
findings.description: An attacker may cause an HTTP/2 endpoint to read arbitrary amounts of header data by sending an excessive number of CONTINUATION frames.

findings.title: Linux PAM Denial of Service in pam_namespace
findings.description: linux-pam (aka Linux PAM) before 1.6.0 allows attackers to cause a denial of service (blocked login process) via mkfifo because the pam_namespace.so open() call will block if the target is a FIFO pipe.

findings.title: Glibc buffer overflow in dynamic loader (Looney Tunables)
findings.description: A buffer overflow was discovered in the GNU C Library's dynamic loader while processing the GLIBC_TUNABLES environment variable, allowing a local attacker to execute arbitrary code with elevated privileges.

findings.title: shadow-utils username input validation flaw
findings.description: In Shadow 4.13, it is possible to inject control characters into fields provided to the SUID program chfn (change finger information). Although it is not possible to exploit this directly, the inserted control characters may allow an unprivileged user to misrepresent who changed a user's information.

findings.title: Rapid stream resets cause excessive work in net/http
findings.description: A malicious HTTP/2 client which rapidly creates requests and immediately resets them can cause excessive server resource consumption.

findings.title: Golang net/html cross-site scripting
findings.description: Text nodes not in the HTML namespace are incorrectly literally valued. Foreign content in the HTML namespace is incorrectly escaped.

findings.title: PostCSS line return parsing error
findings.description: An issue was discovered in PostCSS before 8.4.31. It affects linters using PostCSS to parse external Cascading Style Sheets.

findings.title: GnuTLS timing side-channel in RSA-PSK
findings.description: A vulnerability was found in GnuTLS. The response times to malformed ciphertexts in RSA-PSK ClientKeyExchange differ from response times of ciphertexts with a correct PKCS#1 v1.5 padding.

findings.title: ps buffer overflow
findings.description: Under some circumstances, this weakness allows a user who has access to run the 'ps' utility on a machine, the ability to write almost unlimited amounts of unfiltered data into the process heap.

findings.title: Bash heap buffer overflow in parameter_brace_expand
findings.description: A flaw was found in the bash package, where a heap-buffer overflow can occur in valid parameter_brace_expand.

findings.title: iconv buffer overflow in ISO-2022-CN-EXT
findings.description: The iconv() function in the GNU C Library versions 2.39 and older may overflow the output buffer passed to it by up to 4 bytes when converting strings to the ISO-2022-CN-EXT charset.

findings.title: systemd-resolved stub resolver DNSSEC bypass
findings.description: A vulnerability was found in systemd-resolved. This issue may allow systemd-resolved to accept records of DNSSEC-signed domains even when they have no signature.

findings.title: DNSSEC KeyTrap denial of service
findings.description: Certain DNSSEC aspects of the DNS protocol allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service via DNSKEY and DS records.
